introSection: The Boss is a wooden terrain roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA. The ride was built by Custom Coasters International, Inc.. Opened on April 29, 2000, it was the park's second wooden roller coaster.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was announced in September 1999. Its name was revealed on February 3, 2000.

The Boss was originally planned to open on April 21, 2000, but the opening was delayed to April 29.

For the 2018 season, the final helix was removed which shortened the ride by approximately. The Boss was originally long.

On March 7, 2025, Six Flags St. Louis shared an image of Titan Track from Great Coasters International and Skyline Attractions to be added to the ride. Approximately of Titan Track was added.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride's track uses the existing terrain, which reduces the amount of support structure required.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were built by Gerstlauer.

fullText:
The Boss is a wooden terrain roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA. The ride was built by Custom Coasters International, Inc.. Opened on April 29, 2000, it was the park's second wooden roller coaster.

The roller coaster was announced in September 1999. Its name was revealed on February 3, 2000.

The Boss was originally planned to open on April 21, 2000, but the opening was delayed to April 29.

For the 2018 season, the final helix was removed which shortened the ride by approximately. The Boss was originally long.

On March 7, 2025, Six Flags St. Louis shared an image of Titan Track from Great Coasters International and Skyline Attractions to be added to the ride. Approximately of Titan Track was added.

The ride's track uses the existing terrain, which reduces the amount of support structure required.

The trains were built by Gerstlauer.
